plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate risk.
Trim SMH and CRWD to reduce sector concentration and position size.
Trim SPY to address the concentration flag and increase cash.
Add QQQI to increase income allocation.
Add AMD as a strong long setup for potential growth.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leverage and concentration, creating a large cash buffer.

plan: Trim SOXL to reduce leverage exposure, sell SPAL entirely.
Trim SMH to lower semiconductor concentration.
Trim SPY to address the concentration flag and free up capital.
Add AMD and SNOW, both strong long setups, for growth.
Maintain a balanced approach between broad indices and quality tech.
balanced.read: This plan aims for risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% monthly. It balances broad market exposure with quality tech and limited leverage, while reducing concentration risks.

plan: Increase SOXL and add SPAL, leaning into leveraged ETFs.
Increase SMH to concentrate on semiconductors, a strong scanner read.
Trim SPY to reduce broad index concentration.
Add AMD, RDDT, and NET, focusing on top scanner long setups.
Embrace higher leverage and concentration for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly by leaning into leverage and concentration. It significantly increases upside potential but also magnifies downside risk, with higher drawdowns possible due to leverage and daily ETF rebalancing.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and volatile single names (CRWD, SMH).
Trim SPY to free up capital for income-generating assets.
Significantly increase QQQI to maximize income generation.
Add VST as a stable income-focused setup.
Prioritize steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This plan focuses on generating steady monthly income, targeting around 1% monthly. It cuts leverage and high-beta names to prioritize stable distribution-focused ETFs, aiming for low drawdowns.
